zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Hill於大約1479年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特豪德斯同時，她的父親Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset59歲，母親Alice Stourton16歲。她於大約1490年與Sir Nicholas Wadham在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1557年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特享年79歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特易爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Hill于大约1479年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特豪德斯同时，她的父亲Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset59岁，母亲Alice Stourton16岁。她于大约1490年与Sir Nicholas Wadham在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1557年在英国英格兰薩默塞特享年79岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特易爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Hill was born about 1479, in Houndstone, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset, was 59 and her mother, Alice Stourton, was 16. She married Sir Nicholas Wadham about 1490, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1557, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79, and was buried in Ilton, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Hill est née environ 1479, à Houndstone, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset, avait 59 ans et sa mère, Alice Stourton, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Nicholas Wadham environ 1490, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1557, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Ilton, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Hill um 1479 in Houndstone, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset 59 und ihre Mutter Alice Stourton 16. Sie hat um 1490 in England Sir Nicholas Wadham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1557 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Somerset, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ilton, Somerset, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hillが約1479年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトホーウンドストーンで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somersetは59歳，母Alice Stourtonは16歳であった。約1490年にイギリスでSir Nicholas Wadhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1557年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトで79歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトイルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hill(이)가 대략 1479년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 허운드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset(은)는 59세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Stourton(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1490년 영국에서 Sir Nicholas Wadham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1557년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 이턴에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Nicholas Wadham aproximadamente 1490, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Hill родилась около 1479 г. в местности хоундстоун, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Hill Of Spaxton Somerset было 59 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Stourton, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Nicholas Wadham около 1490 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1557 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности илтон, сомирсет, Англия.
